results.scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
results.justification: All pass criteria met: 8 business rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings (exceeds 5 minimum), 6 escalations serving as clarifying questions (exceeds 5 minimum), 'demand' explicitly identified as undefined in multiple places, acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then written), overall confidence.level is 25 (well under 40 threshold), and no specific numeric values were invented.

observations.text: Excellent handling of ambiguity — correctly identified the CEO statement as a 'vision statement, not a specification'
Empty acceptance_criteria array is exactly right — Bird correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
Confidence level of 25 is appropriately low and well-justified
BR-4 (price never negative) and BR-8 (quoted price honored) show strong domain reasoning — inferring true invariants beyond what was stated
Demand ambiguity surfaced prominently as the first escalation with concrete options for how to define it
Edge cases are excellent — demand feed failure, price flickering, regulatory price-gouging, floor/ceiling misconfiguration all identified
Assumptions are clearly labeled as assumptions, not presented as requirements
Regulatory risk surfaced proactively despite not being mentioned in the input
Minor: testable_assertions on some rules read close to Given/When/Then format, though they appropriately note undefined thresholds rather than inventing values

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context', 'missing_context'

meta.notes: Preliminary scoring by Coach K. Human review pending via HTML report.
